git.notGitRepo: This project is not a Git repository

gitTab.selectProject: Select a project to view git information
gitTab.notGitRepo: This project is not a Git repository
gitTab.confirmDeleteBranch: This will permanently delete the branch.
gitTab.mergeInProgress: Merge in progress — resolve conflicts in the Git tab
gitTab.confirmAbortMerge: Abort the current merge? Uncommitted changes will be lost.
gitTab.confirmRemoveWorktree: Remove this worktree? The directory will be deleted.
gitTab.confirmForceRemoveWorktree: This worktree has uncommitted changes. Force remove?
gitTab.noDiffAvailable: No diff available (new or binary file)
gitTab.confirmDeleteRemoteBranch: Delete remote branch "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
gitTab.tagMessagePrompt: Tag message (leave empty for lightweight tag):

gitErrors.notARepo: This folder is not a Git repository. Initialize one with 'git init'.
gitErrors.notSymbolicRef: The reference is not valid. The branch may have been deleted.
gitErrors.pathNotFound: The specified file or path was not found in the repository.
gitErrors.invalidBranchName: Invalid branch name. Avoid spaces and special characters.
gitErrors.localChangesOverwrite: You have uncommitted changes that would be overwritten. Commit or stash them first.
gitErrors.branchNotMerged: This branch has unmerged changes. Use force delete if you're sure.
gitErrors.remoteRefNotFound: The remote branch was not found. It may have been deleted.
gitErrors.remoteNotFound: Cannot connect to the remote repository. Check the URL.
gitErrors.authFailed: Authentication failed. Check your credentials or token.
gitErrors.networkError: Network error. Check your internet connection.
gitErrors.pushRejected: Push rejected. Pull the latest changes first.
gitErrors.mergeConflict: Merge conflicts detected. Resolve them before continuing.
gitErrors.unrelatedHistories: Cannot merge unrelated histories. The branches have no common ancestor.
gitErrors.lockFailed: Cannot lock the reference. Another git operation may be in progress.
gitErrors.alreadyExists: This already exists. Choose a different name.
gitErrors.nothingToCommit: Nothing to commit. The working tree is clean.

settings.title: Settings
settings.quickActionPresetsDesc: Custom presets shown in the quick actions configuration
settings.reduceMotionDesc: Remove all transitions and animations from the interface
settings.aiCommitMessagesDesc: Use GitHub Models (GPT-4o-mini) to generate commit messages. Requires GitHub connection.
settings.checkForUpdatesDesc: Check if a new version is available
settings.showTabModeToggleDesc: Show the Chat/Terminal switch button on terminal tabs (hover to reveal)
settings.contextPacksDesc: Bundles of files, text, and rules to inject as context in chat
settings.promptTemplatesDesc: Reusable prompt templates with variable substitution
settings.showDotfilesDesc: Show hidden files and folders (starting with a dot) in the file explorer
settings.explorerIgnorePatternsDesc: Additional folder/file names to hide (comma-separated, added to built-in patterns)
settings.telemetryEnabledDesc: Help improve Claude Terminal by sharing anonymous usage data
settings.telemetryCategoryFeaturesDesc: Terminal, chat, git and other feature usage
settings.parallelAutoKanban: Auto-create Kanban cards after Parallel run
settings.parallelAutoKanbanDesc: Create one card per sub-task when a parallel run finishes
settings.parallelAutoKanbanColumnDesc: Column title where cards are created (e.g. Done, Review)
settings.parallelAutoWorkspaceDoc: Write Workspace doc after Parallel run
settings.parallelAutoWorkspaceDocDesc: Save a summary document in the selected workspace KB
settings.parallelWorkspaceIdDesc: Workspace where the summary doc is saved
settings.compactProjectsDesc: Show only the name of unselected projects
settings.cardButtonClaudeDesc: Show the Claude Code shortcut on each project card
settings.cardButtonTerminalDesc: Show a shortcut to open a basic terminal on each project card
settings.modeSafeDesc: Claude asks for confirmation before each action
settings.modeAutonomousWarning: This mode allows Claude to run commands without approval.
settings.accountsDesc: Save the credentials currently active in ~/.claude/.credentials.json so you can switch between them when one hits its usage limit. Run "claude /login" in a terminal to add a new account.
settings.githubConnectDesc: Enter a Personal Access Token to clone private repos
settings.githubHostnameDesc: GitHub hostname used for remote URL detection
settings.personaInstructionsPlaceholder: e.g. Always reply in French, be concise, use informal tone...
settings.personaHelp: Optional. Appended to every new chat session so Claude knows who you are and how to respond.
settings.themesDesc: Choose a theme to customize your terminal colors
settings.tabRenameOnSlashCommandDesc: Automatically rename the terminal tab to the last slash command executed (e.g. /gsd:verify-work 12)
settings.aiTabNamingDesc: Use AI to generate short tab names from messages
settings.enableFollowupSuggestionsDesc: Show AI-generated suggestion chips after Claude responds
settings.discordRpcDesc: Show what you're working on on your Discord profile (needs the Discord app running)
settings.discordRpcShowProjectDesc: Off shows a generic "Using Claude Terminal" without revealing project names
settings.enhancePromptsDesc: Reformulate your prompts with AI for better results before sending (uses Haiku)

hooks.title: Smart Hooks
hooks.description: Real-time insights from Claude Code (time tracking, notifications, dashboard stats)

settings.restoreTerminalSessionsDesc: Restore terminal tabs and Claude sessions from the previous session on startup
settings.enable1MContextDesc: Enable 1 million token context window for Opus and Sonnet (API only, beta)
settings.ephemeralChatsDesc: Don't save conversation transcripts to disk. Sessions cannot be resumed later.
settings.autoClaudeMdUpdateDesc: Suggest CLAUDE.md updates at the end of chat sessions.
settings.confirmTypeChange: The current content will be cleared. Continue?
settings.exportSettingsDesc: Save your settings as a JSON file
settings.importSettingsDesc: Load settings from a JSON file
settings.importError: Failed to import settings: invalid file
settings.rerunSetupDesc: Change language, accent color, editor, and hooks settings

ui.selectMcpForLogs: Select a MCP to view its logs
ui.selectProjectForDashboard: Select a project to view its dashboard
ui.skillAgentHint: The skill/agent will be created in the background using this project's context
ui.describeWhatToCreate: Describe what you want to create and Claude will generate the code for you.

ui.sidebarDragHint: Drag to reorder - Right-click a tab to hide it quickly
ui.tooltipPlugins: Browse and install Claude Code plugins
ui.tooltipTimeTracking: Track time spent on each project
ui.tooltipConnectivity: GitHub, remote access and cloud settings
ui.tooltipErrorLog: Centralized error log with AI triage

memory.panelDescription: Configure how Claude behaves in your projects. Global settings apply everywhere, project settings override them.
memory.createHint: Create a memory file for {name} to customize Claude's behavior.
memory.templateInsertHint: The template will be inserted at the cursor position.
memory.createPrivate: Create private instructions for {name} (not committed to repo).
memory.rulesHint: Define global development rules applied to all projects.

gitChanges.multipleCommitsHint: Files touch {count} areas ({names}). Consider separating into multiple commits.
gitChanges.noChangesHint: Your working tree is clean. Edit files to see changes here.
gitChanges.confirmReset: Undo last commit? Changes will be kept as staged.

skillsAgents.panelDescription: Skills customize how Claude responds. Agents are autonomous workers with their own tools.
skillsAgents.noSkillsHint: Skills are custom instructions that shape how Claude behaves in your projects (coding standards, commit style, review rules...).
skillsAgents.noAgentsHint: Agents are autonomous Claude instances with restricted tools, designed to handle specific tasks independently.

mcpLocal.panelDescription: MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers extend Claude with custom tools like databases, APIs, and file systems.
mcpLocal.noServersHint: MCP servers give Claude access to external tools and data sources. Browse the Registry to discover and install servers.

timetracking.endTooltip: Time of the last coding session today

chat.title: Chat
chat.suggestTodos: Find and fix the {count} TODO(s) in the code [Tab]
chat.suggestGit: Suggest a commit message for the {count} modified files [Tab]
chat.errorRateLimit: Rate limit reached. Please wait a moment before sending another message.
chat.errorBilling: Billing error. Please check your Anthropic account.
chat.errorAuth: Authentication failed. Please log in again by running "claude" in a terminal.
chat.errorMaxTokens: Maximum output length reached. Try asking for a shorter response.
chat.errorServer: Server error. Please try again in a moment.
chat.planReviewPrompt: Review the plan above and approve or request changes.
chat.enteringPlanMode: Claude wants to plan before implementing
chat.permissionNotification: A permission request for {tool} is waiting for your response
chat.slashLoop: Run a prompt repeatedly on interval
chat.dropFolderNotSupported: Folders cannot be attached, drop files instead
chat.mentionFile: Attach a file from your project
chat.mentionNoContextPacks: No context packs found. Create one in Settings > Library.
chat.mentionNoPrompts: No prompt templates found. Create one in Settings > Library.
chat.mentionSymbol: Find and attach a function/class definition
chat.diffRefPrompt: Git ref to diff against (e.g. HEAD~3, main, abc1234):
chat.symbolNamePrompt: Symbol name to find (function, class, variable):
chat.errorMaxTurns: Claude reached the {count}-turn limit. Start a new session to continue.
chat.errorMaxBudget: Budget limit reached (${cost}). Start a new session to continue.

consent.title: Enable Smart Hooks
consent.description: Claude Terminal can integrate with Claude Code using hooks for real-time insights. Hooks detect when Claude starts, stops, or uses tools. All data stays local on your machine.
consent.data2: Tool names used (e.g. file edit, bash)

errors.backupFailed: Could not create backup. Your projects have been reset.

controlTower.emptyDesc: Open a terminal or start a chat to begin.

remote.enableDesc: Control Claude Terminal from your phone, over local Wi-Fi or the cloud
remote.qrHint: Scan with your phone to connect
remote.pinLabel: Enter this code on your mobile device
remote.pinHint: Open the server address on your phone, enter the PIN to connect
remote.networkInterfaceDesc: IP address shown in the server URL
remote.localInfoBanner: Your phone and PC must be on the same Wi-Fi network. Start the server, then scan the QR code or enter the PIN from the mobile app.
remote.helpIntro: Remote control lets you operate Claude Terminal from your phone — run commands, view the terminal, interact with the chat.
remote.helpLocalStep1: Enable remote control and start the server
remote.helpLocalStep2: Make sure your phone is on the same Wi-Fi network as your PC
remote.helpLocalStep3: Scan the QR code with your camera or type the URL in your browser
remote.helpLocalStep4: Enter the 4-digit PIN code shown on screen
remote.helpLocalNote: The PIN expires every 2 minutes for security. No data leaves your local network.
remote.helpCloudStep1: Deploy your own relay server (separate open source project)
remote.helpCloudStep2: Enter the server URL and the API key generated by the admin
remote.helpCloudStep3: Click Connect — the app connects via secure WebSocket (WSS)
remote.helpCloudStep4: Open the web interface from anywhere, the connection goes through your relay
remote.helpCloudNote: No data goes through our servers. The relay is 100% self-hosted — you stay in full control.
remote.helpSecurityPoint2: Encrypted WSS connection for cloud mode
remote.helpSecurityPoint3: No third-party servers — everything stays with you
remote.persistentPinHint: Fixed 6-digit PIN that never expires. Use only on trusted networks.

workspace.title: Workspaces
workspace.deleteConfirm: Delete workspace "{name}"? All knowledge base documents will be permanently removed.
workspace.name: Name
workspace.noWorkspacesHint: Create a workspace to group related projects and build a shared knowledge base.
workspace.enrichToast: Update "{name}" knowledge base with insights from this conversation?
workspace.enrichNoSuggestions: No new insights to add to the knowledge base.
workspace.enrichError: Failed to analyze conversation for workspace enrichment.

errorLog.title: Error Log
errorLog.noErrors: No errors recorded - everything is running smoothly.

cloud.panelDisconnected: Connect to your cloud server to manage projects and sessions.
cloud.apiKeyDesc: Generated by the server admin with ct-cloud user add
cloud.infoBanner: Connect from anywhere via the internet. Requires a self-hosted relay server - no data goes through our servers.
cloud.installHint: Run this on your Linux server - installs Docker automatically, builds the relay, and creates your first user.
cloud.autoUploadConfirmMany: {count} new projects detected. Upload all to the cloud?
cloud.confirmCloudDelete: Remove "{name}" from the cloud? Local files won't be affected.
cloud.conflictDesc: Modified both locally and on cloud
cloud.projectRemovedFromCloudMessage: {count} project(s) no longer exist on the cloud: {name}. Delete them locally?
cloud.resetHint: Clears sync metadata and upload status. Use if cloud sync is stuck or out of sync.
cloud.resetMessage: This will clear all sync metadata and upload tracking. Projects and files are not affected. You may need to re-upload projects afterward.

database.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this connection?
database.noConnectionsHint: Add a connection or use auto-detect to discover databases in your project
database.noDetected: No databases detected in this project
database.name: Name
database.allowDestructiveTooltip: Allow DROP, DELETE FROM, TRUNCATE, ALTER queries
database.selectTable: Select a table to browse its data

database.browserNoRows: No rows found in this table
database.browserDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this row?
database.browserInsertEmpty: Fill in at least one field
database.browserEditNotSupported: Inline editing is not supported for MongoDB
database.pendingDiscardMessage: You have pending cell edits that will be lost. Discard them?
database.deleteSelectedConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete {count} row(s)?
database.redisSelectKey: Select a key to view its value

telemetry.consentDescription: We'd like to collect anonymous usage data to help improve Claude Terminal. This is completely optional and can be disabled at any time in Settings.
telemetry.consentChangeSettings: You can change this setting at any time in Settings.
telemetry.collect4: Country and city (via IP geolocation, IP is never stored)

workflow.scopeHint: Which projects this workflow can run on
workflow.concurrencyHint: Behavior when the workflow is already running

webhook.label: Webhook (HTTP POST)
webhook.urlHint: POST this URL with Authorization: Bearer <your-api-key>
webhook.noCloud: Cloud relay not configured — connect in Settings → Cloud
webhook.payloadHint: The request body is available as $trigger.payload in your workflow nodes.
webhook.saveForUrl: Save the workflow to get the URL

project.listHint: Returns an array of all Claude Terminal projects. Connect the output to a Loop node's Items slot to iterate.

claude.cwdHint: Working directory for the Claude session
claude.promptPlaceholder: Analyze this file and summarize the changes...
claude.argsHint: Text passed to the skill as argument

trigger.hookTypeHint: Claude event that triggers the workflow
trigger.hookPreToolUse: Pre Tool Use — Before each tool call
trigger.hookPostToolUse: Post Tool Use — After each tool call
trigger.hookProjectHint: Only fire when the hook event comes from this project (matched by working directory).
trigger.hookToolNameHint: Comma-separated tool names (supports * wildcards). Leave empty to match all tools. Only applies to PreToolUse / PostToolUse.
trigger.manualHint: This workflow starts manually via the ▶ button or via the API.
trigger.webhookHint: This workflow is triggered via an HTTP POST from an external service (GitHub, Stripe, etc.).
trigger.fileChangeProjectHint: Watch this project's directory (leave empty to use a custom path)
trigger.fileChangePathHint: Absolute path to watch. Overrides the project selection if set.
trigger.fileChangePatternsHint: Optional glob relative to the watched folder (e.g. **/*.js). Leave empty to watch everything.
trigger.fileChangeDebounceHint: Wait this long after the last change before firing.
trigger.terminalExitCustomHint: Fire when the exit code matches one of these values (e.g. 1,2,127).
trigger.terminalExitProjectHint: Limit to terminals opened from this project.
trigger.terminalExitCommandHint: Optional. Match against the spawned command (substring match, or /regex/). Example: "claude" or "/npm (run|test)/".
trigger.projectOpenedHint: Fire when this project is opened. Leave on "Any project" to match all.
trigger.claudeSessionStartHint: Fire when a Claude chat session starts in this project.
trigger.claudeSessionEndHint: Fire when a Claude chat session ends in this project.
trigger.claudeSessionStatusHint: Filter by how the session terminated.
trigger.gitEventProjectHint: Watch this project's local repository (.git folder).
trigger.gitEventTypeHint: Which git operation should fire the workflow?
trigger.gitEventBranchHint: Optional. Only fire on this branch (exact name) or /regex/. Example: "main" or "/^feat\\//".
trigger.chatMessageProjectHint: Fire only for Claude sessions in this project.
trigger.chatMessagePatternHint: Optional text / regex to match. Leave empty to match every message.

loop.sourceHint: Where the items to iterate come from
loop.itemsHint: One item per line, or variable $node_X.rows
loop.schemaHint: Use these paths in nodes inside the loop body
loop.schemaEmpty: Connect an array source or run once to see the schema
loop.usageTip: Connect a node to the Each port to process each item

time.actionProject: Project — stats for a specific project
time.actionAllProjects: All projects — sorted by today's time
time.sessionsProjectHint: Empty = global sessions, otherwise sessions for a project

sql.tip: Tip: use {{variable}} to inject dynamic values

subworkflow.workflowHint: Workflow to run as a subprocess
subworkflow.waitHint: Block execution until the subworkflow ends

list.emptySub: Automate your repetitive tasks with Claude

create.title: New workflow
create.blankSub: Start from an empty canvas and build it yourself
create.aiSub: Describe what you want — AI builds the workflow for you
create.promptPlaceholder: e.g. Every morning at 9am, pull the latest changes on all my projects, run tests and notify me if any fail.
create.promptHint: Be specific: trigger, steps, error handling, notifications…

history.selectRun: Select a run to see details
history.clearMessage: Delete {n} runs from history? This action is irreversible.

editor.selectNode: Select a node to configure its settings
editor.insertVariableNode: Click to insert a Variable node

loopSuggest.text: This link carries an array. Insert a Loop?

marketplace.mineEmptySub: Share your workflows with the community!
marketplace.descriptionPlaceholder: This workflow does X, Y, Z…
marketplace.publishInfo: Your workflow will be immediately visible in the Explore tab.

mock.dailyCodeReview: Daily code review by Claude. Runs a full analysis at 8am, fixes issues, and pushes if tests pass.
mock.autoTestOnCommit: Triggered after each Git tool call. Runs tests automatically and notifies on failure.
mock.prSummaryAgent: Generates a PR summary via Claude and posts it as a GitHub comment through the API.
mock.deployNotifyDiscord: Complete deployment pipeline with Discord notification.
mock.changelogAutoWriter: Generates CHANGELOG.md from Git commits via Claude.
mock.securityAuditWeekly: Weekly audit: vulnerable dependencies, exposed secrets, permissions.

folder.hint: Base directory to explore (empty = current project)

content.hint: Text or data to write to the file

project.label: Target project

column.label: Column
column.hint: Column id or title (empty = first column)

goal.label: Goal
goal.hint: Feature to split into parallel sub-tasks

attempts.label: Max attempts
attempts.hint: Total number of tries before giving up (first attempt included).

delay.label: Base delay (ms)

backoff.hint: How the delay grows between retries.

webhook.hint: Paste the full Discord webhook URL.

content.label: Message
content.hint: Plain text content. Supports variables like $project.name.

parallel.subtitle: Split a feature into multiple tasks and run them simultaneously. Requires a git repository.

form.goalPlaceholder: Describe the feature to implement in parallel (e.g. "Add OAuth2 login with Google and GitHub providers")...

merge.prPushFailed: Failed to push branch to remote

merge.prOpenedBrowser: Branch pushed — opening PR page in browser

errors.noGit: This project is not a git repository. Parallel tasks require git for branch isolation.
